symptoms.symptom_name: Migratory pain: periumbilical to fixed right iliac fossa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Migratory periumbilical-to-RLQ pain is one of the most specific historical features for appendicitis (LR+ ~3.2).
Mesenteric adenitis pain is typically diffuse and less reliably migratory in this classic pattern.
Anorexia is present in the large majority of appendicitis cases; a normal appetite argues against the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain worsened by movement, coughing, and jolting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Historical proxy for peritoneal irritation (equivalent to a positive cough/hop test), obtainable without examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade subjective fever and one episode of vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low-grade fever and vomiting following the onset of pain are typical secondary features.
Absence of dysuria, frequency, or urgency reduces UTI probability substantially.

symptoms.symptom_name: Involuntary guarding and rebound tenderness at McBurney's point, positive Rovsing's/psoas/obturator signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic peritoneal signs localising to McBurney's point; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Enlarged non-compressible appendix >6mm with periappendiceal fat stranding on imaging

investigation_details.expected_result: Enlarged non-compressible appendix >6mm with periappendiceal fat stranding
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis remains unconfirmed; the telehealth AI Doctor cannot order imaging directly and should recommend it occur as part of ED assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging confirmation is the definitive pre-operative diagnostic criterion.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of migratory pain, anorexia, peritoneal-type aggravation by movement, and low-grade fever/vomiting constitutes a high-probability appendicitis presentation warranting urgent surgical pathway activation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Abdominal ultrasound or CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line imaging to confirm appendicitis and guide surgical planning; cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, CRP, urinalysis, beta-hCG (if applicable)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports diagnosis, excludes pregnancy-related causes where relevant, and assesses inflammatory response.
Plain abdominal X-ray has poor sensitivity for appendicitis and would inappropriately delay definitive cross-sectional imaging.
